text.title: Greenfield Infra Decision Matrix Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Greenfield Infra Decision Matrix methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Matrix MUST cover these axes: compute-platform, container-orchestrator, IaC-tool, regions, data-store, CI-CD, observability, secrets, identity. Custom axes added with justification.
rule.rationale: Fixed axes ensure no critical decision is omitted; ad-hoc axes drift.

rule.statement: Red-flags (compliance, vendor lock-in, team skill gap) MUST disqualify cells automatically, before scoring. Red-flag = no_go regardless of points.
rule.rationale: Scoring over red-flags hides constraints; explicit no_go keeps the decision honest.

rule.statement: Cells (after red-flag filter) scored 1-5 per axis with 1-paragraph rationale per cell. Weights documented before scoring (no post-hoc tuning).
rule.rationale: Post-hoc weighting biases to the desired answer; up-front weights anchor honesty.

rule.statement: Decision record MUST include rationale per chosen cell + alternatives considered + reason rejected. 'Because Reasons' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Future engineers need the rationale to re-evaluate; opaque decisions calcify.

rule.statement: Decision record MUST be revisited yearly or at scale events (10x growth, region expansion). Stale records flagged for re-evaluation.
rule.rationale: Cloud capabilities + team skills evolve; stale records keep teams on suboptimal choices.

rule.statement: Decision record signed by engineering leader + tech-lead + security lead. Unsigned records non-binding.
rule.rationale: Signoff makes the decision a contract, not a draft.
